title: Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS) Protocol
description: Emergency protocol for suspected heart attack, angina, or acute coronary events.
protocol: Administer aspirin 325mg (chew). Obtain 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes. Initiate IV access. Administer supplemental O2 if SpO2 < 94%. Activate cardiac catheterization lab if STEMI confirmed.
correction_reason: Chest pain with radiation and diaphoresis warrants CRITICAL severity per ACS protocol.

title: Stroke (CVA) Protocol
description: Emergency protocol for suspected stroke — FAST assessment.
protocol: Note symptom onset time. Perform FAST assessment. CT scan within 25 minutes of arrival. tPA within 3-4.5 hours of onset if eligible. Monitor airway and oxygenation.
correction_reason: Acute stroke symptoms require CRITICAL severity − time-critical window for thrombolysis.

title: Anaphylaxis Protocol
description: Emergency protocol for severe allergic reactions.
protocol: Administer epinephrine IM 0.3-0.5mg (adults). Position patient supine with legs elevated. Establish IV access. Administer H1/H2 antihistamines. Prepare for potential intubation.
correction_reason: Anaphylaxis is a CRITICAL emergency requiring immediate epinephrine administration.

title: Major Trauma Protocol
description: Emergency protocol for significant traumatic injuries.
protocol: Primary survey: ABCDE approach. Control hemorrhage with direct pressure/tourniquet. Immobilize C-spine if mechanism suggests. Establish large-bore IV access. Prepare for surgical intervention.
correction_reason: Major trauma with hemorrhage or altered consciousness requires CRITICAL severity.

title: Severe Burn Protocol
description: Emergency protocol for significant burn injuries.
protocol: Cool burn under running water for 20 minutes. Cover with clean non-adherent dressing. Calculate TBSA using Rule of Nines. Initiate fluid resuscitation per Parkland formula for >15% TBSA. Manage pain with IV morphine.
correction_reason: Burns >10% TBSA or involving face/hands/genitalia warrant HIGH severity per ATLS.

title: Chest Pain Triage Guidelines
description: General triage guidelines for patients presenting with chest pain.
protocol: Obtain vital signs including SpO2. 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes. Troponin level. Chest X-ray. Assess for PE, pneumothorax, aortic dissection. Risk stratify using HEART score.
correction_reason: All chest pain presentations should be at least HIGH severity until cardiac cause is excluded.

title: Acute Respiratory Distress Protocol
protocol: Assess SpO2 and respiratory rate. Position upright. Administer supplemental O2 to maintain SpO2 > 94%. Nebulized bronchodilators for bronchospasm. Prepare for non-invasive ventilation or intubation.
correction_reason: Acute respiratory distress with SpO2 < 92% or inability to speak warrants HIGH severity.

title: Fracture Management Protocol
description: Protocol for managing suspected bone fractures.
protocol: Immobilize in position found. Apply ice and elevation. Assess neurovascular status (5 Ps). X-ray. Closed reduction or surgical referral as needed. Pain management with NSAIDs or opioids.
correction_reason: Open fractures or fractures with neurovascular compromise require upgrade to HIGH severity.

title: Acute Abdominal Pain Protocol
description: Triage protocol for acute abdominal presentations.
protocol: Assess vitals and pain characteristics. NPO status. Labs: CBC, BMP, lipase, urinalysis, pregnancy test. CT abdomen if indicated. Surgical consult for acute abdomen signs.
correction_reason: Abdominal pain with rigidity or hemodynamic instability warrants upgrade to HIGH/CRITICAL.

title: Minor Wound Care Protocol
description: Guidelines for minor cuts, abrasions, and superficial wounds.
protocol: Clean wound with saline or clean water. Apply antiseptic. Cover with appropriate dressing. Assess tetanus immunization status. Educate on signs of infection.
correction_reason: Minor wounds are LOW severity unless signs of significant infection are present.
